discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -42,6 +43,12 @@ def get_auth_headers(
     profile: str | None,
     data: str | None,
 ) -> dict[str, str]:
+    bedrock_bearer = os.getenv("AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK")
+    if bedrock_bearer is not None:
discussion_comments.comment_body:
I think `if bedrock_bearer:` would be better here because it's only `True` when `bedrock_bearer` is non-empty while this line can be `True` for the empty string.

> In the context of Boolean operations, and also when expressions are used by control flow statements, the following values are interpreted as false: False, None, numeric zero of all types, and empty strings ...
https://docs.python.org/3/reference/expressions.html#boolean-operations

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -42,6 +43,12 @@ def get_auth_headers(
     profile: str | None,
     data: str | None,
 ) -> dict[str, str]:
+    bedrock_bearer = os.getenv("AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK")
+    if bedrock_bearer is not None:
discussion_comments.comment_body:
An empty string is a valid value for AWS environment variables, and the AWS SDK uses the `AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK` environment variable, even when it's empty.

So this code just matches the AWS SDK behaviour:

https://github.com/boto/botocore/issues/3603

discussion_comments.comment_body: Good catch @karpetrosyan\! You're right, the `response is not None` check is redundant since we already return early if it's `None` on the line above. Removed it in both the sync and async `__run__` methods. Thanks for the review\!
